MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL A. MULLER, TEL. NO. (925) 944-9700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ILENE GOLDBERG OF CARR & FERRELL, TEL. NO. (650) 817-3400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS RECOLOGY INCORPORATED AND GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The Court denies summary judgment. The Court denies and grants summary adjudication in part. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the first cause of action for negligence because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached its duty of care. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the trespass cause of action because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff had a possessory interest in the tree. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the nuisance cause of action. Plaintiff's principal claim is based on the view and any problems regarding noise were incidental. See Oliver v. AT&T Wireless (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 521. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the NIED cause of action because it is not a distinct cause of action, see Potter v. Firestone Tire & Rubber Co., (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 984, and emotional distress damages are not recoverable for pure property damage. See Cooper v. Superior Court (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 1008, 1012. The Court grants as to the IIED cause of action because there is no evidence of outrageous conduct. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Angie Diner CSR # 9581 =302/eg
